title: Introduction
description: Title slide — AuRen AI Dev Platforms 2024–2026 overview with API-style request/response
speakerNotes: Welcome to AuRen — a comprehensive research report comparing 10 AI-autonomous development platforms. We cover autonomy, self-healing loops, and full-stack deployment capabilities across the 2024-2026 era.

title: Platform Landscape
description: Overview of all 10 platforms with type badges and key differentiators
speakerNotes: The landscape spans from fully autonomous cloud agents like Devin, to open-source CLI tools like Codex CLI, to enterprise-grade assistants like Gemini Code Assist. Three major new platforms joined in 2025.

title: Key Metrics
description: Aggregate KPI stats across 10 platforms — self-healing count, open source, automation average
speakerNotes: Key metrics: 10 platforms tracked, 3 open-source, 8 with self-healing loops, average automation level 8.3/10. The 2025 wave pushed open-source agent count from 1 to 3.

title: Devin AI
description: Deep dive: Cognition's autonomous agent — first AI Software Engineer
speakerNotes: Devin remains the benchmark for full autonomy at 10/10. Version 2.0 in 2025 added multi-agent team coordination and enterprise PR review automation.

title: Copilot Workspace
description: Deep dive: GitHub Copilot Workspace — Microsoft's issue-to-PR pipeline
speakerNotes: Copilot Workspace differentiates through deep GitHub integration — turning issues directly into PRs with planning-first workflow. 1.8M enterprise subscribers as of 2025.

title: Replit Agent
description: Deep dive: Replit Agent — full-stack prompt-to-deploy cloud IDE
speakerNotes: Replit Agent uniquely serves non-developers with no-code interface while maintaining full-stack capability. One-click deploy with persistent NixOS runtime is unmatched.

title: Cursor
description: Deep dive: Cursor — local-first VS Code fork with multi-model AI
speakerNotes: Cursor's 900K MAU in 2025 validates the local-first, developer-centric approach. Composer with multi-agent support and background cloud agents solidifies its enterprise position.

title: Bolt.new
description: Deep dive: Bolt.new — StackBlitz WebContainers full-stack in the browser
speakerNotes: Bolt.new's multi-agent switchboard and 10M monthly users demonstrate that zero-setup browser-based development has gone mainstream. WebContainers technology is the key enabler.

title: v0.dev
description: Deep dive: v0.dev — Vercel's React/Next.js frontend generator
speakerNotes: v0.dev occupies a clear niche: UI-first, image-to-code, Vercel ecosystem. Limited self-healing but unmatched for frontend velocity with Vercel deployment in one click.

title: OpenClaw
description: Deep dive: OpenClaw (AuRen) — self-hosted, privacy-first, MIT-licensed agent
speakerNotes: OpenClaw (now branded AuRen) serves regulated industries requiring on-premise deployment. Multi-model routing with Ollama enables local LLM inference for maximum data privacy.

title: Claude Code
description: NEW 2025: Anthropic Claude Code — terminal-native agent with constitutional AI and 200K context
speakerNotes: Claude Code launched April 2025 and achieved the fastest enterprise adoption in Anthropic history. Constitutional AI safety guardrails are a first-in-class feature preventing insecure code generation.

title: OpenAI Codex CLI
description: NEW 2025: OpenAI Codex CLI — open-source terminal agent, 60K GitHub stars in 30 days
speakerNotes: Codex CLI's MIT license and 60K GitHub stars in 30 days signal a massive shift toward open-source agentic tools. The sandboxed shell execution model is safe for production use.

title: Gemini Code Assist
description: NEW 2025: Google Gemini Code Assist — 1M token context, enterprise-grade GCP integration
speakerNotes: Gemini Code Assist's 1M token context is a category-defining capability. Entire monorepos fit in a single context window, eliminating chunking errors and enabling truly holistic refactoring.

title: The 2025 Agent Wave
description: Analysis: How Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Gemini Code Assist reshaped the landscape in 2025
speakerNotes: The 2025 wave brought agent specialization, open-source explosion, 1M context wars, and constitutional AI safety to production. These aren't incremental updates — they're category-defining shifts.

title: Pipeline
description: Automation Pipeline — 5-stage Generate → Run → Detect → Patch → Rerun loop
speakerNotes: The automation pipeline now spans 10 platforms with 9 supporting automated testing and 8 supporting full self-healing loops. The 2025 additions pushed coverage to near-universal.

title: Self-Healing Loop
description: Self-healing debug loop — mechanics, pseudocode, and verification pattern
speakerNotes: Self-healing is now the baseline expectation. 8 of 10 platforms implement some form of detect-patch-verify loop, with constitutional AI guardrails emerging as the next differentiator.

title: Feature Matrix
description: 9-dimension feature matrix comparing all 10 platforms side-by-side
speakerNotes: The feature matrix shows clear segmentation: enterprise-grade (Devin, Bolt, OpenClaw) vs. specialist (Claude Code, Codex CLI) vs. UI-focused (v0.dev). No single platform wins every dimension.

title: Comparison Scores
description: Platform comparison scores across 6 dimensions with bar charts
speakerNotes: Claude Code leads in Code Quality (10/10). Devin leads in Autonomy and Self-Healing. Gemini Code Assist leads in Collaboration and Infrastructure. Each platform has a distinct strength profile.

title: Autonomy Spectrum
description: Four-level autonomy spectrum — where each platform sits from completion to full autonomy
speakerNotes: The autonomy spectrum has shifted up. In 2024, only Devin sat at Level 4 (full autonomy). By 2026, Claude Code and Bolt.new operate at the same tier in specific domains.

title: Deployment Models
description: Three deployment models: Cloud SaaS, Embedded IDE, Self-Hosted
speakerNotes: Self-hosted deployments grew from 1 platform (OpenClaw) to 3 (plus Codex CLI) in 2025. Privacy-first deployment is no longer a niche requirement — it's becoming enterprise standard.

title: Multi-Agent Arch
description: Multi-agent orchestration pattern — Planner, Coder, Tester, Reviewer roles
speakerNotes: Multi-agent architectures are now supported by 6 of 10 platforms. The Planner → Coder → Tester → Reviewer pattern has emerged as the de facto standard for autonomous software engineering.

title: Trends 2024–2026
description: Four defining trends across the AI-autonomous development era
speakerNotes: The four mega-trends: Agent Emergence (2024), Self-Healing as Standard (2024-25), Multi-Model + Local LLMs (2025), Infra Provisioning as Feature (2025-26).

title: Key Takeaways
description: Three key takeaways — autonomy commoditization, data residency, frontend specialization
speakerNotes: Three takeaways: 1) Autonomy is commoditizing — self-healing is now table stakes. 2) Data residency drives self-hosted adoption. 3) Frontend and full-stack are diverging into distinct platform categories.
